145 - Sales Enthusiasm - Does It Actually Help- - Sales Training with Jeremy Miner - YouTube.mp4
Now in this video, you're gonna learn how your excitement and your enthusiasm about what you're selling could be losing sales for you. Now, you've always heard the saying from the old sales gurus that you have to be excited about your product or service. You have to be so enthusiastic. Show your customer about how excited you are, and that's going to make you a great salesperson. Now, in the post-trust era that we live in, with trust being at its lowest point in the history of America, being excited just doesn't work anymore. Unless you want to keep getting average results and you cannot assume that because you are excited and enthusiastic about what you're selling that your potential customers are just going to be excited as well.
Remember, their motivations for buying something or making a change is going to be completely different from yours or other prospects that you have. Now, one of the biggest reasons that so many sales people stay average and they stay broke is because they approach their potential prospects with too much external enthusiasm. Now, one of two things will happen when you do this. Number one, your potential customers will withdraw from you because you have overwhelmed them with your enthusiasm. So they don't return your calls. They don't respond to your emails or text messages. They don't show up for your appointments. They just vanish on you. Does that ever happen to you? Or, number two, they throw out objections and reject you because of the sales pressure your excitement has caused.
You see, they look at you as just another salesperson. You're no different than anybody else that's trying to stuff your solution down their throat. Let me give you an example of this. Let's say that your car breaks down and you literally have to go out and buy a car that weekend so you can go to work on Monday. So you're in the market to buy a car right now. You go to a car dealership intended on buying a car that day, and the salesperson comes out and says, 'Let's take a look at the script.' Hey, how can I help you today? Mr. and Mrs. Jones, the prospect says, 'Well, we're just looking for a new car.' Salesperson says, 'Well, you know, we've got some great promotions going on this weekend, just amazing promotions.
You know, we've got the best deals in town. We've got the best finance rates as well.' Prospect says, 'Well, we're just kind of looking for something affordable, actually.' Salesman says, 'Oh, well, let me tell you, we've got some great cars here at our dealership. We can beat any deal in town. I just love working for this dealership. In fact, I just came over from Chevy about three months ago. Just so much better over here. Just the customer service alone, how people feel about the car, and I know you're going to be so happy when you buy this car from us, Mr. and Mrs. Jones. Now, let me show you this car over here. Come on over here. I really think that you'll like the safety features on this car for your kids, and it's got even air-conditioned seats.
Here's the problem.' The salesperson just keeps assuming that they're going to buy, just because they keep explaining the features, the benefits that he or she thinks they want. They keep throwing mud on the wall, hoping some of it sticks. Now let me ask you: What do you usually do if a salesperson is really excited and enthusiastic when they're talking with you? You're right. You put up your defensive wall. You throw out objections. And you reject the salesperson and get rid of them at any cost. So have you ever considered that if you're enthusiastic and you're excited, that you could be causing your potential customers to do the exact same thing to you? How true is that, right?
You know, sometimes it's hard when we start to realize that we were the ones that actually caused our potential customers to reject us and throw out objections. Now, the seven-figure salesperson does not show his or her external enthusiasm because they know it would automatically trigger sales resistance from their prospects. They are internally excited about what they are selling. Obviously, you can be excited, but be excited on the inside, not on the outside. The seven-figure salesperson will actually downplay him or herself to their potential customer. This will make people come to your rescue and want to help you because they will trust you and not look at you as just a dreaded salesperson. Now this is a skill that I'm also going to give you access to at another time. Now I want to thank you for watching this video about how your enthusiasm and your excitement about what you're selling can actually be losing you sales and how you can change that forever. Now in the next video, we're going to discuss how to effectively start a meeting and a presentation to automatically draw in your prospects to what you're offering.
